author: Security Bot
message:
test: Fix remaining test failures across suites

- HttpConnectionIOCoordinatorTests: Fix parser timeout by providing correct HTTP headers for protocol errors and fix async race condition in MockIOConnection.
- PDSWebSocketTransportTests: Fix sync assertions against async methods and update MockNetworkConnection to queue receives correctly.
- PDSWebSocketServerTests: Update port and handler assertions to correctly await asynchronous setup.
- SubscribeReposHandlerTests: Provide valid CID string for blob array to fix NSInvalidArgumentException nil insertion.

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(appview): resolve CID links in DAG-CBOR record blocks

Add recursive CID link resolution in AppViewIngestEngine to follow IPLD
references in firehose CAR blocks. Firehose blocks often contain CID links
(CBOR tag 42) that reference other blocks containing the actual record data.

Changes:
- Add ResolveCIDLinksInObject() to recursively resolve CID references
- Prefer decodeDataAsJSON() for better CID->$link wrapper handling
- Use blockWithCID:() for direct lookup instead of looping
- Check for actual record fields instead of just non-empty dicts
- Add detailed debug logging for CID resolution tracking

This fixes the issue where graph records were appearing as empty dicts
because their actual data was in linked blocks that weren't being
followed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(appview,sync): harden CAR parsing and implement persistent handle resolution

Summary:
- Refactored CARReader to use spec-compliant varint-based CID parsing.
- Updated CID.m to support CIDv0 and robust CIDv1 binary decoding.
- Updated AppView indexers to use provided CIDs and DAG-CBOR codec.
- Added persistent 'handles' table to AppViewDatabase for fast handle-to-DID resolution.
- Integrated handle discovery into firehose ingest and backfill worker.
- Verified 100% pass rate (1648/1648 tests).

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(relay): capture weak delegates strongly to prevent SIGBUS crashes

Relay was crashing with EXC_ARM_DA_ALIGN on ARM64 when weak delegate
properties were accessed in dispatch_async blocks without strong capture.
The delegate could be deallocated between block creation and execution,
causing an alignment fault when messaging garbage memory.

Fixed in RelayClient.m (6 instances):
- notifyDisconnectionWithError:
- firehoseSubscriptionDidConnect:
- firehoseSubscription:didReceiveCommitEvent:
- firehoseSubscription:didReceiveIdentityEvent:
- firehoseSubscription:didReceiveErrorEvent:
- firehoseSubscription:didCloseWithError:

Fixed in RelayUpstreamManager.m (6 instances):
- relayClient:didReceiveCommitEvent:
- relayClient:didReceiveIdentityEvent:
- relayClient:didReceiveErrorEvent:
- relayClientDidConnect:
- relayClient:didDisconnectWithError:
- relayClient:didReceiveCursor:

Pattern: Capture delegate strongly with local variable before async block
or before direct call, then check for nil before messaging.

Added debugging skill for future reference.

🤖 Generated with Letta Code (https://letta.com)

Co-Authored-By: Letta Code <noreply@letta.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(relay): fix URL scheme detection for localhost upstreams

NSURL parses "localhost:2583" with scheme="localhost" which is wrong.
Now check for "://" presence first before URL parsing to correctly
detect missing scheme, then prefix with http:// or https://.
